question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ContractResult (gasConsumed field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract call where `gasConsumed` is set to a value that is exactly equal to `gasLimit`, causing downstream tools to incorrectly classify the transaction as having exhausted all gas (out-of-gas) when it actually succeeded, triggering incorrect fee-penalty logic?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ContractResult (transactionResult field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction that results in a success code (22) but with `gasUsed` equal to `gasLimit`, causing the mirror node to persist a `ContractResult` that appears to have succeeded but consumed all gas, confusing fee-refund calculations that expect a refund for unused gas?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ContractResult (gasConsumed field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract call where the sidecar `gasUsed` at `call_depth=0` is negative (if the protobuf allows it), causing `calculateGasConsumed` to produce a `gasConsumed` value less than the intrinsic gas cost, underreporting the fee?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ContractResult (functionParameters field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract call with `functionParameters` that is a valid ABI-encoded call to a high-gas function, causing the intrinsic gas calculation to be dominated by the per-byte cost of the parameters rather than the execution cost, making `gasConsumed` an inaccurate proxy for the actual fee?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ContractResult (gasLimit field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with `gasLimit` set to a value that is valid for the Ethereum transaction type but exceeds the Hedera network's maximum, causing the mirror node to persist an out-of-bounds `gasLimit` that the REST API exposes without validation?
